business.geography_hint: London, UK (Bethnal Green, Vauxhall, Homerton, Stoke Newington, Stratford, Tottenham, Barking)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking PLACES — physical taprooms/bottleshops with location cards, per-venue feature taglines and downloadable beer/food menus; browsing the venues and their menus IS the decision, so place over any thing/trust argument.

goal.one_action: get visitors to pick a nearby venue (view its menu) and/or sign up to the newsletter to stay in the loop

structure.archetype_note: WordPress multi-location venue site built on blog-post templates; sprawling informational hub (locations, menus, events, jobs, merch) rather than a single tight funnel — primary explicit CTA is the outbound newsletter signup.

mapped.observed_as: nav (About Us, Locations, Merch, Events, Jobs)
Mother Kelly's Taprooms & Bottleshops brand banner
(location cards) grid linking to per-venue pages
1 Railway Arch ∙ 22 Taps ∙ Craft Drinks ∙ 6 Huge Fridges (per-venue tagline)
social links (Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, Linktree)

unmapped.observed: View Beer Menu / View Food Menu — per-location downloadable PDF menus
unmapped.why_no_fit: Menus are location-specific downloadable PDF files, not an on-page menu-list; no canonical section for offer-as-downloadable-asset

unmapped.observed: VACANCIES — job listings (Supervisor, General Manager, Bartenders...)
unmapped.why_no_fit: Careers/recruiting listings serve a secondary audience (job seekers); no canonical section for job vacancies

unmapped.observed: Events — blog-style feed of dated event posts (e.g. Pohjala party at Vauxhall)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A dated schedule of one-off venue events; no canonical section captures an events feed

proposed_new.name: external-list-signup
proposed_new.evidence: Primary newsletter CTA 'SIGN UP TO OUR NEWSLETTER' routes off-site to a Mailchimp subscribe page rather than an on-page form

proposed_new.name: event-listings
proposed_new.evidence: Events page is a blog-style feed of individual dated event posts per venue

confidence.why: Capture is full, but static-HTML extraction is thin (no h1s, no embedded menu content, no map embed) so per-venue detail and hours are under-represented; design tone (only Roboto/photo-heavy, no colors/dark-light) is ambiguous, so design_style is inferred.

gap_findings: Multi-location venue business has NO on-page map/hours and NO booking/reservation — the 'place' engine assumes visitable-location info (address, hours, directions, map) that this capture lacks; map-hours is needed but absent.
Offer (the menu) is delivered as per-location downloadable PDFs, not an expressible menu-list — no canonical section or capability for offer-as-downloadable-asset.
Goal is genuinely diffuse across newsletter / menu-view / events / jobs / merch-store redirect; the single-conversion-action model can't cleanly represent a multi-audience venue hub.
Careers/vacancies is a recurring secondary-audience page type (like press-kit/jobs) with no taxonomy home.
